behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because this failure appears at module handoff (3 collectors -> aggregation payload -> summary step input). PASS2-AUDIT: Unit could miss wiring omissions between modules; System rejected because behavior text only requires that "content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources" (no requirement for real network/OS/subprocess); Agentic rejected because this checks pre-LLM input payload, not model judgment; Workflow rejected because no multi-agentic sequence. FINAL: Integration. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) real OS/network/subprocess needed? No. (b) real AI/ML output needed? No. (c) multiple agentic steps? No. (d) LLM-stage orchestration correctness dependent on model decision? No.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real aggregation/orchestration modules with stubbed source clients returning distinct markers and assert the exact payload handed to summary-generation contains all three source sections/values.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, since resilience behavior spans source adapter error -> report assembly fallback section text. PASS2-AUDIT: Unit may miss cross-module propagation (exception handling plus section rendering); System rejected because behavior text is about fallback semantics ("`data unavailable` note ... rather than aborting"), which can be triggered with in-process failing adapter; Agentic rejected because no requirement depends on model output quality; Workflow rejected because no multi-agentic pipeline. FINAL: Integration. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) real OS/network/subprocess needed? No, simulated source failure is sufficient. (b) real AI/ML output needed? No. (c) multiple agentic steps? No. (d) LLM-governed stage handoff? No.
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with two source modules returning valid data and one source module raising an availability error; verify the report completes and includes a `data unavailable` placeholder for only that section.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Agentic, because correctness is about summary content fidelity, which depends on real model generation behavior. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration with deterministic stubs would hide hallucination/omission errors; Workflow rejected because behavior references a single summary-generation judgment, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence; System is insufficient alone because real infra without real model-judgment evaluation still misses the target failure. FINAL: Agentic. REJECTION-GATE for Workflow: disqualified by behavior text focusing on one step, "The narrative summary accurately represents..." (not a multi-step agent pipeline).
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real summary-generation model on representative inputs and score/assert factual alignment of generated narrative against key numeric/status values (including omission and contradiction checks) across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: System, because this behavior explicitly requires end-to-end delivery to a real inbox boundary. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration could validate orchestration with mocked email, but that can miss SMTP/API/auth/routing/inbox-delivery failures; Agentic rejected because success criterion is delivery to inbox, not model output quality (behavior text: "reaches ... inbox correctly"); Workflow rejected because it does not require multiple agentic decisions. FINAL: System.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scheduled script in a production-like environment against reachable services and a real test mailbox, then assert the email is received by configured recipients with expected report structure/content envelope.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, as failure handling spans delivery module failure, filesystem persistence, and next-run retry state handoff. PASS2-AUDIT: System considered due to "service is unreachable," but disqualified by behavior text that focuses on application fallback semantics (save + retry) which can be reproduced with an in-process failing mail client and real local filesystem; Agentic rejected because no model-quality dependency; Workflow rejected because no multi-agentic sequence; Unit may miss cross-run persistence wiring. FINAL: Integration. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) real OS/network/subprocess needed? Real network no; local filesystem yes, still within Integration. (b) real AI/ML output needed? No. (c) multiple agentic steps? No. (d) LLM-stage orchestration? No.
behaviors.test_description: Use real local filesystem state and a mail client double that fails first send, run scheduler-triggered job twice, assert first run writes pending report artifact and second run retries and clears/marks it after successful send.
